opp.ais_why:
• AI importance is rated 'Neutral' and the Merlin product line is empty - the only opportunity in this batch with no Merlin or AI products tagged. The product scope is strictly eProcurement and eInvoicing, a traditional P2P RFI without AI modules.
• The opportunity description and next-step history detail a procedural Ariba-based RFI - Tender Launch March 11, demos March 23-27, proposal deadline March 27 - with no buyer-stated AI requirements. The RFI is framed as "streamlining the procurement and payment process", which is a functional rather than AI-led mandate.
• Only one Avoma meeting was recorded - the April 9 2026 demo with 15 OXXO attendees - and it is in a not-recorded state with no transcript or notes to mine. As of the May 12 next-step note, OXXO is still evaluating internally with no AI-specific traction. Confidence is medium because the non-AI scope is clear even without recordings.

sources: SF NextStep field March 6-June 16 (streamlining procurement/payment process)
SF Products__c: eProcurement;eInvoicing
Known risk from prior sweep: no ROI case or quantified business impact
metrics.narrative: OXXO articulated operational pain around payment processing inefficiency and tax validation complexity, but has not quantified the business impact. The RFI/RFP process (launched March 11, NextStep field) describes streamlining procurement and payment processes, and the Products scope (eProcurement + eInvoicing, SF Products__c field) confirms automation of payment portal and invoicing workflows. However, no buyer quote states a savings target, cycle time reduction, headcount impact, or cost baseline. The April 9 demo was not recorded, so no transcript exists to mine for quantified pain. NextStep May 12 notes 'oxxo still evaluating internally' with no mention of ROI drivers. Zycus is competing on functional fit alone without a data-driven value case anchoring the deal to a CFO or finance sponsor.

sources: SF OpportunityContactRole: Melissa Balderas and Ali Nassif marked Evaluator, no Champion role assigned
SF NextStep March-June: all interactions transactional, no advocacy signals
Ground truth: Melissa Balderas recent task contact
No Avoma calls to assess champion behavior
champion.narrative: No champion identified. Melissa Balderas (IT Analyst - Software Procurement) is the primary Zycus contact (marked Evaluator in Contact Roles, recent task contact per ground truth), but there is no evidence of advocacy, internal influence, or access to decision-makers. The NextStep history shows Melissa and Ali Nassif (Category Manager Software, also Evaluator) as the buyer contacts, but all interactions are transactional: RFI submission, demo scheduling, clarification requests, and the June 8 reschedule announcement. No Contact Role is marked Champion, and no call transcript exists to assess whether Melissa or any other contact is actively selling Zycus internally, sharing competitive intel, or pushing back on competitors. The reschedule announcement (June 8) and subsequent 27-day silence suggest Melissa either lacks the authority to restart the process or is not invested in accelerating it. Senior stakeholders (Patricia Fabila, David Leal Garza, Gloria Campos) are in the contact roster but have not engaged directly with Zycus based on available evidence. Without a champion, Zycus is operating blind in a multi-vendor evaluation.

sources: SF NextStep March 11, March 27: multi-vendor RFI/RFP process
SF NextStep May 26: Mexico customer reference inquiry (competitive vetting signal)
Q3 SOQL: Competitors__c, Others_Competitors_Please_specify__c, Existing_vendor__c returned no data
No Avoma calls discovered
competition.narrative: OXXO is running a multi-vendor RFI/RFP process (NextStep March 11, March 27), but no competitor names are documented in Salesforce (Competitors__c, Others_Competitors_Please_specify__c, Existing_vendor__c all returned no data in Q3 SOQL) and no Avoma calls exist to capture competitive mentions. The NextStep field describes parallel vendor evaluation sessions (implied by 'use-case review sessions' plural and the Ariba multi-vendor RFI process), and the April 9 demo was not recorded, so no transcript is available to mine for competitive intelligence. The only indirect signal is the May 26 NextStep entry: 'Oxxo inquired about our customers in mexico - fu with customers w operations in mexico plus decero / tigo & added cbc', which suggests OXXO is vetting Zycus's Mexico market presence, potentially comparing it to a local or regional competitor. The competitive blind spot is severe: Zycus does not know who else is presenting, what their positioning is, or how OXXO is scoring vendors. This gap, combined with the lack of champion and EB, means an invisible competitor with executive access could be outflanking Zycus.

sources: SF NextStep March 6: 'streamlining the procurement and payment process'
SF Products__c: eProcurement;eInvoicing
Known requirement: use-case presentations on Payment Reference, Tax Validation, Volume Analysis, Reports/KPIs
SF OpportunityContactRole: Patricia Fabila (Finance Transformation), Luis Kauil (Tax Tech), David Leal Garza (Procurement)
identify_pain.narrative: OXXO is in the process of streamlining procurement and payment processes, which is the foundational pain driving this RFI (NextStep March 6: 'They are in process of streamlining the procurement and payment process and invited us to participate in RFI process'). The Products scope (eProcurement + eInvoicing) and the four requested use-case topics (Payment Reference, Tax Validation, Volume Analysis, Reports/KPIs) confirm operational pain around manual payment processing, tax compliance complexity, and lack of procurement visibility/reporting. The pain owner is Finance and Procurement: Patricia Fabila (Gerente de Transformación Administración y Finanzas), Luis Kauil (Gerente Fiscal Tax Tech), and David Leal Garza (Senior Procurement Manager) are all on the contact roster, indicating finance-transformation and procurement-optimization mandates. However, the pain is not quantified (no stated savings, cycle time, or error-rate target), and the specific inefficiencies (e.g. manual invoice matching, tax calculation errors, slow AP cycles) are inferred from the use-case topics rather than quoted from a buyer conversation.

sources: SF NextStep March 6, March 9: NDA signed with redlines, legal documentation provided
SF NextStep March 6: Ariba onboarding (procurement channel)
SF OpportunityContactRole: Javier Espinosa (Infraestructura Cloud) suggests InfoSec involvement
Q2 SOQL: Current_Contract_Expiration__c returned no data
paper_process.narrative: Legal and InfoSec mechanics are partially visible but incomplete. OXXO required an NDA before RFI participation; Zycus signed with redlines (NextStep March 6, March 9). The buyer also requested legal documentation for their internal review (March 6, March 9), which Zycus provided. Procurement is channeled through Ariba (onboarded March 6), and the contact roster includes an InfoSec/infrastructure role (Javier Espinosa, Resp. Infraestructura Cloud), suggesting InfoSec review is likely but not yet documented. No MSA, SOW, or contracting timeline has been discussed; the deal is still in RFI/demo/use-case phase with no commercial conversation. Current_Contract_Expiration__c was not populated in Q2 SOQL, so there is no incumbent-contract forcing function visible. What is missing: MSA/SOW template requirements, legal review timeline, InfoSec questionnaire status, procurement approval thresholds, and whether an SI or implementation partner is involved (none named in contacts or NextStep).

sources: SF OpportunityContactRole query: 15 contacts, none with Economic Buyer or Executive Sponsor role
SF NextStep April 14-May 26: scope inquiry but no pricing/commercial discussion
No Avoma calls discovered (see evidence_coverage)
economic_buyer.narrative: No Economic Buyer identified. The 15-person contact roster includes finance roles (Patricia Fabila Rubio, Gerente de Transformación Administración y Finanzas; Luis Kauil Contreras, Gerente Fiscal Tax Tech; Lizbeth Carreño Gochez, Business Transformation Finance) and a Senior Procurement Manager (David Leal Garza), but none are marked as Economic Buyer or Executive Sponsor in Contact Roles, and no Avoma calls exist to verify who controls budget and pricing authority. All contacts are Evaluators or technical/category analysts. NextStep history shows no commercial or pricing conversation; the only budget-adjacent signal is the April 14 inquiry about PO generation module scope, which Karson answered with a sizing request (PO volume, user count) that the buyer never fulfilled. No evidence of who will approve the $250k spend or sign the contract. The absence of an EB 269 days from close and 121 days into the cycle is a critical gap.

sources: SF NextStep March 6-June 16: Ariba onboarding, RFI submission, demo, clarifications, reschedule announcement
SF OpportunityContactRole: procurement, IT, and finance roles present but no approval hierarchy documented
Q3 SOQL: Does_the_Buyer_need_approval__c, Purchase_approvals_Required_from__c, Multiple_approvals__c returned no data
decision_process.narrative: OXXO is running a structured, multi-vendor RFI/RFP process managed through Ariba (onboarded March 6, per NextStep). The sequence to date: NDA signed March 6, RFI released March 11, Zycus submitted RFI response March 27 via Ariba, demo delivered April 9, additional clarifications submitted April 10-15, and use-case review sessions scheduled then rescheduled June 8. The process is procurement-led (contact roster includes IT Analyst Software Procurement Melissa Balderas, Senior Procurement Manager David Leal Garza, Category Manager Ali Nassif), with IT coordination (Raúl Segovia, Gloria Campos) and finance representation (Patricia Fabila, Luis Kauil, Lizbeth Carreño). However, the approval chain is unknown: no Salesforce field (Does_the_Buyer_need_approval__c, Purchase_approvals_Required_from__c, Multiple_approvals__c) was populated, and no call transcript exists to clarify escalation steps or final sign-off authority. The current stall (buyer-initiated reschedule, no new dates for 27 days) suggests internal prioritization or budget uncertainty, but Zycus has no visibility into what is blocking progression or when the evaluation will resume.

sources: SF NextStep March 11, March 27: RFI/RFP released, demo requested
Known requirement: use-case presentations on Payment Reference, Tax Validation, Volume Analysis, Reports/KPIs
SF Products__c: eProcurement;eInvoicing
Q3 SOQL read returned no data for Business_Requirements__c, Top_Challenges_Priorities__c, AI_Needs_in_RFP_Rating__c
decision_criteria.narrative: OXXO's RFI/RFP (released March 11, per NextStep) and the planned use-case review sessions signal structured evaluation criteria, but the criteria themselves are not documented in Salesforce or Avoma. The Products scope (eProcurement + eInvoicing) and the four use-case topics OXXO requested for presentation — Payment Reference, Tax Validation, Volume Analysis, Reports/KPIs (known requirement from prior sweep) — imply functional priorities around tax compliance, payment automation, and reporting. However, no Salesforce field (Business_Requirements__c, Top_Challenges_Priorities__c, AI_Needs_in_RFP_Rating__c) was populated in the Q3 SOQL read, and the April 9 demo was not recorded. The reschedule of use-case sessions (June 8) means the detailed criteria review has not yet occurred. What is confirmed: OXXO is running a multi-vendor RFI with parallel sessions and expects Spanish-language presentations (NextStep March 27). What is missing: weightings, must-haves vs nice-to-haves, how AI/Merlin factors in, and how Zycus is scored vs competitors.

cro_panel.intro: One read per score, in language a CRO/CO can act on. We're behind on this one — the reads below say why and what would change it.

blocks.read: We're well behind — this one is cold.
blocks.title: Zycus win position

bullets.full: Losing on capability fit — No AIS field data available. AIS_Score__c, AIS_Status__c, and AIS_Why__c returned no values in Q2 SOQL. No Avoma calls to infer AI appetite from buyer language. The use-case scope (eProcurement, eInvoicing, tax validation, payment automation) is traditional process automation with no stated AI requirement. However, OXXO is a large enterprise (FEMSA subsidiary, major retail/convenience chain) undergoing digital transformation (finance transformation, procurement streamlining), which typically signals openness to AI-driven automation if positioned correctly. Tier is unknown; assume AI Curious until buyer appetite is confirmed.
bullets.text: Losing on capability fit — No AIS field data available.

bullets.full: Buyer is leaning elsewhere — No AI positioning signal available. AIS_Score__c, AIS_Status__c, and AIS_Why__c were not populated in Q2 SOQL read. No Avoma calls exist to assess whether OXXO has AI appetite or whether Merlin/agentic procurement narrative has been presented. The use-case topics (Payment Reference, Tax Validation, Volume Analysis, Reports/KPIs) are traditional procurement/finance automation, with no explicit AI or intelligent-automation mention. Zycus may be under-positioning Merlin differentiation in a market where AI-native competitors could be advancing.
bullets.text: Buyer is leaning elsewhere — No AI positioning signal available.

bullets.full: No real champion yet — No champion identified. Melissa Balderas is the primary contact but shows no advocacy, urgency, or internal influence. Senior stakeholders (Patricia Fabila, David Leal Garza, Luis Kauil, Gloria Campos) are on the roster but not engaged. Without a champion, Zycus has no visibility into internal dynamics, competitive positioning, or decision-maker priorities.
bullets.text: No real champion yet — No champion identified.

bullets.full: No executive access yet — No Economic Buyer identified. The 15-person contact roster includes finance roles (Patricia Fabila Rubio, Gerente de Transformación Administración y Finanzas; Luis Kauil Contreras, Gerente Fiscal Tax Tech; Lizbeth Carreño Gochez, Business Transformation Finance) and a Senior Procurement Manager (David Leal Garza), but none are marked as Economic Buyer or Executive Sponsor in Contact Roles, and no Avoma calls exist to verify who controls budget and pricing authority. All contacts are Evaluators or technical/category analysts. NextStep history shows no commercial or pricing conversation; the only budget-adjacent signal is the April 14 inquiry about PO generation module scope, which Karson answered with a sizing request (PO volume, user count) that the buyer never fulfilled. No evidence of who will approve the $250k spend or sign the contract. The absence of an EB 269 days from close and 121 days into the cycle is a critical gap.
bullets.text: No executive access yet — No Economic Buyer identified.

bullets.full: Edge over the competition — Zycus is competing in a multi-vendor RFI/RFP with no visibility into competitor names, positioning, or OXXO's scoring criteria. The May 26 Mexico reference inquiry suggests OXXO is vetting regional market fit, possibly comparing Zycus to a local or incumbent vendor. Without a champion, EB access, or recorded calls, Zycus cannot differentiate or counter competitive narratives.
bullets.text: Edge over the competition — Zycus is competing in a multi-vendor RFI/RFP with no visibility into competitor names, positioning, or OXXO's scoring criteria.

bullets.full: ROI / business case landed — OXXO articulated operational pain around payment processing inefficiency and tax validation complexity, but has not quantified the business impact. The RFI/RFP process (launched March 11, NextStep field) describes streamlining procurement and payment processes, and the Products scope (eProcurement + eInvoicing, SF Products__c field) confirms automation of payment portal and invoicing workflows. However, no buyer quote states a savings target, cycle time reduction, headcount impact, or cost baseline. The April 9 demo was not recorded, so no transcript exists to mine for quantified pain. NextStep May 12 notes 'oxxo still evaluating internally' with no mention of ROI drivers. Zycus is competing on functional fit alone without a data-driven value case anchoring the deal to a CFO or finance sponsor.

Procurement is channeled through Ariba (onboarded March 6), and the contact roster includes an InfoSec/infrastructure role (Javier Espinosa, Resp. blocks.title: What moves it forward

commentary.deal_risk: Risk 17 is driven by Verdict 'At Risk' — weak forward intent. Only observed negatives count, so these are real warning signs, not gaps.
commentary.win_position: Win 29 is carried by competitive strength +0.20 (weight 15); business case strength +0.30 (weight 10). Offsetting it: differentiation strength -0.40 (weight 20).
commentary.deal_momentum: Momentum 54 (roughly flat): Next Step carries 1 dated milestone(s) — actively worked. Silence alone was not treated as backward motion.
commentary.evidence_coverage: Partial Read: 4 of 7 evidence dimensions seen (engagement, exec and champion, commercial, forward motion). It labels how much of the picture we have, never the primary scores.
commentary.customer_commitment: Commitment 51 reflects real customer investment: Customer owns 2 open commitment(s); Security/procurement review underway.
commentary.forecast_confidence: Forecast confidence 21 is anchored on the stage win (29), nudged by commitment 51 and momentum 54, and dragged by risk 17. Read is Partial Read — a confidence flag (we know less), not a haircut on the score.

contributions.evidence: Next Step carries 1 dated milestone(s) — actively worked.

However, OXXO is a large enterprise (FEMSA subsidiary, major retail/convenience chain) undergoing digital transformation (finance transformation, procurement streamlining), which typically signals openness to AI-driven automation if positioned correctly. Tier is unknown; assume AI Curious until buyer appetite is confirmed.

items.detail: Single-threaded through Melissa Balderas (IT Analyst) with no executive sponsor or Economic Buyer mapped. Senior stakeholders (Finance, Procurement leadership) are not engaged. Risk of invisible competitor with executive access outflanking Zycus.

Competitive blind spot: multi-vendor RFI with parallel evaluation sessions but no visibility into competitor names, OXXO's vendor preferences, or how Zycus is differentiated. April 9 demo was not recorded, so no transcript to mine for competitive intelligence.

Buyer-initiated reschedule has stalled use-case sessions for 31 days (since June 8) with no new dates provided. Deal owner confirmed holding pattern June 16. Risk of silent disqualification or deprioritization.

No commercial conversation has occurred. Buyer inquired about PO generation module scope April 14, Karson requested sizing inputs (PO volume, user count), but buyer has not provided. eProcurement scope expansion is unpriced and uncommitted. No ROI case or quantified business impact documented.

No ROI case or quantified business impact. OXXO articulated operational pain (payment processing inefficiency, tax validation complexity) but has not stated savings target, cycle time improvement, or cost baseline. Without a data-driven value case, Zycus is competing on functional fit alone.

Senior stakeholders (Patricia Fabila, David Leal Garza, Luis Kauil, Gloria Campos) are on the roster but not engaged. items.commitment: Provide new dates for use-case review and formal presentation sessions
Provide PO volume and user count to scope eProcurement module

items.action: Executive connect: the deal owner's manager escalates to OXXO's Senior Procurement Manager (David Leal Garza) or Finance Transformation leader (Patricia Fabila Rubio) to restart momentum, confirm OXXO's timeline and priority, and map the Economic Buyer and Decision Maker. Objective: break the 31-day stall, multi-thread above IT analyst level, and gain visibility into internal dynamics and competitive landscape.
items.trigger: Buyer-initiated reschedule June 8 with no new dates provided; deal owner confirmed holding pattern June 16; 31-day silence
items.expected_effect: Restarts buyer engagement within 7 days, confirms OXXO's commitment to evaluation, and surfaces Economic Buyer or Executive Sponsor. If OXXO is still engaged, new use-case session dates are scheduled by end of July. If deprioritized, accelerates disqualification decision and frees resources.

items.action: Deal team: run competitive discovery call with Melissa Balderas and Ali Nassif. Ask directly: who else is in the RFI, what are OXXO's top 3 vendor-selection criteria, how is Zycus scored vs competitors, and what would make Zycus the clear choice. If buyer will not share, escalate to David Leal Garza or Patricia Fabila for transparency.
items.trigger: Multi-vendor RFI process (March 11 RFP release, March 27 Ariba submission) with zero competitive intelligence captured; no competitor names, no scoring visibility, no recorded demo
items.expected_effect: Surfaces 1-2 competitor names, OXXO's decision criteria, and Zycus's competitive position within 10 days. Enables tailored differentiation and wedge strategy for use-case sessions.

items.action: Deal team: secure a finance or procurement champion. Target Luis Kauil Contreras (Tax Tech leader, key for Tax Validation use case) or Lizbeth Carreño Gochez (Business Transformation Finance, likely ROI owner). Schedule a value-discovery call to quantify pain (current tax error rate, manual invoice cycle time, AP headcount), build an ROI case anchored to their transformation KPIs, and recruit them as internal advocate.
items.trigger: No champion identified; Melissa Balderas is transactional only; no internal advocate to share competitive intel, push for restart, or sponsor Zycus to Economic Buyer
items.expected_effect: Identifies a finance or tax champion by end of July who advocates for Zycus internally, shares decision-maker priorities, and builds data-driven ROI case to elevate commercial conversation from functional fit to strategic value.

items.action: Deal team: position Merlin AI differentiation if OXXO has AI appetite. Prepare a targeted Merlin demo or use-case narrative (e.g. AI-powered tax validation, intelligent payment matching, predictive spend analytics) and present it to the relevant stakeholder (Tax Tech) or Patricia Fabila (Finance Transformation) as a Phase 2 or differentiator in the RFI scoring. Confirm whether AI/intelligent automation is a stated or latent requirement.
items.trigger: No AIS signal; use-case topics are traditional automation with no AI mention; risk of under-positioning Merlin in a market where AI-native competitors may be advancing
items.expected_effect: Confirms OXXO's AI appetite within 2 weeks. If AI Hungry or Curious, Merlin becomes the wedge in use-case sessions and differentiates Zycus from legacy competitors. If AI Resistant, avoids wasting cycles on AI narrative.

items.action: Deal team: prepare and send a concise re-engagement email to Melissa Balderas and Ali Nassif summarizing Zycus's Mexico customer proof points (Decero, Tigo, CBC shared May 26), reconfirming readiness to deliver Spanish-language use-case presentations, and requesting a 15-minute call to confirm OXXO's timeline and next steps. CC David Leal Garza or Patricia Fabila to escalate visibility.
items.trigger: 31-day silence since June 8 reschedule; no buyer response to July 2 rep activity
items.expected_effect: Restarts two-way communication within 5-7 days. If buyer responds, confirms new use-case session dates and re-engages evaluation. If no response after CC to senior stakeholders, confirms deal is deprioritized and triggers disqualification discussion.

confidence_signals.summary: Deal is cooling. Last verified Salesforce activity July 2 (3 days ago, rep-side task) with no two-way buyer engagement since June 8 reschedule announcement. Buyer-initiated 31-day stall with no restart commitment. Single-threaded contact (Melissa Balderas) shows no urgency. No champion, no EB, no commercial momentum.

north_star_verdict.math: Created March 6, 2026; 121 days old, 269 days to close March 31, 2027. Stage Formal Evaluation since creation. Buyer requested demo April 1 (delivered April 9), then rescheduled use-case sessions June 8 with no new dates provided. Deal owner confirmed holding pattern June 16. Last activity July 2 (3 days ago). Required pace: move to Shortlisted within 60 days, Vendor Selected by Q4 2026, Negotiation by Q1 2027 to hit close date. Currently 31 days behind schedule since reschedule announcement. No forward slip in CloseDate yet, but stall risk is high. Time-in-stage exceeds typical Formal Evaluation window (30-45 days) by 2.5x.
evidence: LastActivityDate 2026-07-02 (3 days ago) shows rep activity, but no two-way buyer engagement since June 8 reschedule announcement
NextStep June 16: 'still holding pattern waiting on scheduling' — buyer has not provided new dates for 27 days
No Economic Buyer, Decision Maker, or Executive Sponsor identified in 15-person contact roster; all roles are Evaluator or technical/procurement analysts
No champion evidence: no advocate pushing internally, no shared competitive intel, no access to decision-makers
Pipeline forecast is indefensible for a deal 269 days out with no EB, no DM, no commercial conversation, and a 31-day buyer-initiated stall
north_star_verdict.headline: Stalled 31 days in use-case presentations with no buyer commitment to reschedule; single-threaded through IT analyst; no Economic Buyer or Decision Maker mapped 269 days from close

flags: Multi-thread to executive level before rescheduled use-case sessions. Melissa Balderas is a reliable process contact but lacks commercial authority. Engage the relevant stakeholder (Finance, Gerente de Transformación) and David Leal Garza (Senior Procurement Manager) on the business case, ROI quantification, and approval process to secure a champion with Economic Buyer access and competitive positioning insight.

Discover the competitive shortlist and OXXO's vendor preferences immediately. The RFI is multi-vendor with parallel use-case sessions, but Zycus has no visibility into who the competitors are or how OXXO is evaluating them. When the buyer reschedules, ask Melissa or the evaluation team about the vendor shortlist, OXXO's prior procurement platform experience, and Zycus's differentiation in the evaluation to inform positioning strategy.

Quantify the ROI case before the formal presentation. OXXO has articulated operational pain (payment processing inefficiency, tax validation complexity, reporting gaps) but no savings target or current-state cost. Gather invoice volume, payment cycle time, exception rate, and FTE cost from Finance and Procurement stakeholders to anchor a data-driven value case and differentiate on business impact, not just functional fit.

Single-threaded through Melissa Balderas (IT Analyst - Software Procurement). No Economic Buyer, Decision Maker, or Executive Sponsor engaged. Senior finance and procurement stakeholders on contact roster but not active.
No champion identified. No evidence of internal advocate, competitive intel sharing, or decision-maker access. Melissa Balderas is transactional contact only.

Buyer-initiated 31-day stall (reschedule announced June 8, no new dates provided, deal owner confirmed holding pattern June 16). Risk of silent disqualification or competitor advancing while Zycus waits.
No Economic Buyer or commercial conversation 121 days into cycle, 269 days from close. Pipeline forecast is indefensible.
Competitive blind spot: multi-vendor RFI with no competitor names, no visibility into OXXO's scoring, no recorded demo to mine for differentiation signals.
No ROI or quantified business case. Pain is confirmed (streamlining procurement/payment) but not quantified (no savings, cycle time, or cost baseline stated).
best_practice_check.summary: Critical gaps: no Economic Buyer or Decision Maker mapped 269 days from close; single-threaded through IT analyst; no champion; 31-day buyer-initiated stall with no restart commitment; no commercial or ROI conversation; competitive blind spot in multi-vendor RFI.

competitive_position.summary: Zycus is competing in a multi-vendor RFI/RFP with no visibility into competitor names, positioning, or OXXO's scoring criteria. The May 26 Mexico reference inquiry suggests OXXO is vetting regional market fit, possibly comparing Zycus to a local or incumbent vendor. Without a champion, EB access, or recorded calls, Zycus cannot differentiate or counter competitive narratives.

competitors.name: Unknown multi-vendor RFI participants
competitors.quote: March 27 NextStep: 'RFI Submitted via Ariba'; March 11: 'RFP released'; June 8: 'sessions scheduled for the review and presentation of use cases will be rescheduled' (plural sessions implies multiple vendors)

ai_positioning_strength.summary: No AI positioning signal available. AIS_Score__c, AIS_Status__c, and AIS_Why__c were not populated in Q2 SOQL read. No Avoma calls exist to assess whether OXXO has AI appetite or whether Merlin/agentic procurement narrative has been presented. The use-case topics (Payment Reference, Tax Validation, Volume Analysis, Reports/KPIs) are traditional procurement/finance automation, with no explicit AI or intelligent-automation mention. Zycus may be under-positioning Merlin differentiation in a market where AI-native competitors could be advancing.

customer_expectations_fit.summary: OXXO expects Spanish-language use-case presentations covering Payment Reference, Tax Validation, Volume Analysis, and Reports/KPIs. Zycus has not yet delivered these (rescheduled June 8, no new date). The eProcurement scope expansion inquiry (April 14) remains unaddressed due to buyer's failure to provide sizing inputs. Fit is unconfirmed until use-case sessions occur.

hard.ais_why:
• AI importance is rated 'Neutral' and the Merlin product line is empty - the only opportunity in this batch with no Merlin or AI products tagged. The product scope is strictly eProcurement and eInvoicing, a traditional P2P RFI without AI modules.
• The opportunity description and next-step history detail a procedural Ariba-based RFI - Tender Launch March 11, demos March 23-27, proposal deadline March 27 - with no buyer-stated AI requirements. The RFI is framed as "streamlining the procurement and payment process", which is a functional rather than AI-led mandate.
• Only one Avoma meeting was recorded - the April 9 2026 demo with 15 OXXO attendees - and it is in a not-recorded state with no transcript or notes to mine. As of the May 12 next-step note, OXXO is still evaluating internally with no AI-specific traction. Confidence is medium because the non-AI scope is clear even without recordings.
hard.next_step: 6.16.26 KK - still holding pattern waiting on scheduling6.8.26 KK -from OxxO "Through this message, we would like to inform you that the sessions scheduled for the review and presentation of use cases for the Payment Portal project will be rescheduled."Once we have the new dates defined, we will share them with you through this same channel.5.26.26 KK - Oxxo inquired about our customers in mexico - fu with customers w operations in mexico plus decero / tigo & added cbc5.12.26 KK - oxxo still evaluating internally4.15.26 KK - submitted additional responses and clarifications 4.104.1.26 KK - demo scheduled April 93.27.26 KK - RFI Submitted via Ariba with additional supporting documents shared via email. OXXO requested demo April 9th. Confirming if they are ok with english3.12.26 KK - devika assigned, requested extension to clarification questions to tuesday, 17th EOD instead of tomorrow3.11.26 KK - NDA in progress, RFP released. Waiting on internal resource alignment 3.9.26 KK - shared requested legal info 3.6.26 KK - NDA in progress with redlines, onboarded Zycus in Ariba, Waiting on legal to provide necessary documentation for their legal review  BD: They are in process of streamlining the procurement and payment process and invited us to participate in RFI process, shared NDA also for signature.

value.quote: we would like to request your support in presenting several use cases. The use cases to be reviewed include: Payment Reference (Folio de Pago), Tax Validation (Validacion Fiscal), Volume Reference Analysis (Análisis de referencia de volumetría), Reports and KPIs. Please note that both sessions will be conducted in Spanish, so we kindly ask you to take this into consideration when preparing your team.

value.quote: March 27 NextStep: 'RFI Submitted via Ariba'; March 11: 'RFP released'; June 8: 'sessions scheduled for the review and presentation of use cases will be rescheduled' (plural sessions implies multiple vendors)
value.how_we_win: Map the Economic Buyer and Decision Maker immediately; secure a finance or procurement champion who can share competitive intel and internal scoring; position Merlin AI differentiation if OXXO has AI appetite (no AIS signal available); deliver Mexico customer proof points (Decero, Tigo, CBC shared May 26) to de-risk regional deployment concerns

packets.subject: Unknown multi-vendor RFI participants

value.detail: Single-threaded through IT analyst with no Economic Buyer, Decision Maker, or Executive Sponsor mapped. Senior stakeholders (Patricia Fabila, David Leal Garza, Gloria Campos, Luis Kauil) are on contact roster but not engaged. Risk of invisible competitor with executive access outflanking Zycus.
value.source: SF OpportunityContactRole (15 contacts, none EB/DM/Exec Sponsor), no Avoma calls

value.flag: No Economic Buyer or commercial conversation 121 days into cycle, 269 days from close. Pipeline forecast is indefensible.

packets.subject: No Economic Buyer or commercial conversation 121 days into cycle, 269 days from close. Pipeline forecast is indefensible.

value.flag: Competitive blind spot: multi-vendor RFI with no competitor names, no visibility into OXXO's scoring, no recorded demo to mine for differentiation signals.

packets.subject: Competitive blind spot: multi-vendor RFI with no competitor names, no visibility into OXXO's scoring, no recorded demo to mine for differentiation signals.

value.flag: No ROI or quantified business case. Pain is confirmed (streamlining procurement/payment) but not quantified (no savings, cycle time, or cost baseline stated).

packets.subject: No ROI or quantified business case. Pain is confirmed (streamlining procurement/payment) but not quantified (no savings, cycle time, or cost baseline stated).

gaps: No Avoma calls discovered via opp_id (006P700000UuasL, 15-char) or account_id discovery. April 9 demo was not recorded. Zero call transcripts to mine for MEDDPICC depth, competitive intel, or buyer pain quantification.
AIS fields (AIS_Score__c, AIS_Status__c, AIS_Why__c) returned no data in Q2 SOQL. No AI appetite signal.

Q3 synthesis-source fields returned no data: Competitors__c, Business_Requirements__c, Top_Challenges_Priorities__c, AI_Needs_in_RFP_Rating__c, Decision_Maker_Name_Title__c, X10a_Sponsor__c, X10b_Champion_Business_Buyer__c, Does_the_Buyer_need_approval__c, Purchase_approvals_Required_from__c, Multiple_approvals__c, Current_Contract_Expiration__c. These are hygiene gaps (MEDDPICC knowledge exists in NextStep, contact roles, and use-case scope) NOT knowledge gaps, except for competitor names and approval chain (genuinely unknown).

No completed Tasks or Events with competitive intel, commercial discussion, or buyer commitments captured in Description field (Q1 returned standard fields only, tasks/events query not detailed in available evidence).
No quantified ROI or business case: savings target, cycle time baseline, cost reduction KPI genuinely unknown across all sources.
validation gate: a named person in a to-do/action item is in no Salesforce contact role, Avoma attendee list, or active-user roster and is anchored to no source (offending: 'Luis Kauil')
